mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly supporting higher S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic > 1.96 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when macro regime shifts to monetary tightening or recession.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation triggers expectations of imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market health, supporting consumer spending and positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential (p > 0.05) compared to baseline periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion, directly boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals an immediate supply-side shock or demand collapse, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% monthly.
